title: spendable_notes: 'as u64' casts violate established try_from pattern
description: r.value_zatoshi as u64 and r.block_height as u64 in spendable_notes(). The stated codebase pattern (bd memory DESIGN-sqlite-amount-cast) uses i64::try_from(u64) for all zatoshi values to produce a descriptive error rather than a silent cast. The read direction inverts this: a negative value in the DB (e.g., from external corruption) silently wraps to a huge u64 with no diagnostic. The insert side prevents negative values via i64::try_from, so this is latent rather than exploitable today — but the inconsistency means future contributors see two different patterns and will follow the wrong one. Fix: replace both casts with u64::try_from(r.value_zatoshi).map_err(...) and u64::try_from(r.block_height).map_err(...).
close_reason: igs.2: replaced 'as u64' casts with u64::try_from() + descriptive error per established pattern. igs.3: corrected 'eight' → 'nine' in SAFETY comment; medium-term unsafe replacement deferred to separate issue
